# Effective TypeScript (Dan Vanderkam)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Getting to Know TypeScript](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/01-getting-to-know-typescript.md): When the AI is writing, modifying, reviewing, or configuring TypeScript code; setting up or adjusting `tsconfig.json` configurations; migrating JavaScript codebases to TypeScript; or debugging discrepancies between static types and runtime behavior.
    - [TypeScript’s Type System](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/02-typescript-s-type-system.md): The set of all possible runtime values that conform to a specific type.
    - [Type Inference and Control Flow Analysis](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/03-type-inference-and-control-flow-analysis.md): Writing, modifying, reviewing, or refactoring TypeScript code (`.ts`, `.tsx`), specifically when handling variable declarations, type annotations, object initialization, control flow logic, asynchronous functions, and generic function signatures.
    - [Type Design](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/04-type-design.md): Tasks involving creating, refactoring, designing, or reviewing TypeScript type definitions, interfaces, function signatures, state models, classes, or handling data payloads from external sources/APIs.
    - [Unsoundness and the any Type](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/05-unsoundness-and-the-any-type.md): Writing, evaluating, or refactoring TypeScript code, specifically in scenarios involving dynamic data structures, compilation error resolution, global object modification, public API design, and ensuring type soundness and safety.
    - [Generics and Type-Level Programming](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/06-generics-and-type-level-programming.md): TypeScript generics, type-level programming, conditional types, mapped types, template literal types, type testing, and creating reusable generic utilities and library type definitions.
    - [TypeScript Recipes](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/07-typescript-recipes.md): When interpreting TypeScript user requests, analyzing TypeScript code files, or generating TypeScript architectures that involve type safety, control flow analysis, object iteration, API design, variadic functions, union types, or primitive type distinction.
    - [Type Declarations and @types](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/08-type-declarations-and-types.md): This rule file is activated when the AI is modifying package.json dependencies, writing or consuming TypeScript type declaration files (.d.ts), configuring TypeScript build tools, designing or modifying public APIs, documenting TypeScript code, writing callbacks that bind the this context, or managing third-party library typings.
    - [Writing and Running Your Code](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/09-writing-and-running-your-code.md): Writing, modifying, debugging, or analyzing TypeScript code, configuring TypeScript build tools (tsconfig.json, Webpack, Vite, ts-node), authoring tests for TypeScript projects, implementing runtime data validation, or writing code that interacts with the browser DOM or Node.js runtime environment.
    - [Modernization and Migration](.roo/vendor/typescript/effective-typescript-dan-vanderkam/10-modernization-and-migration.md): Migrating legacy JavaScript codebases to TypeScript and modernizing outdated JavaScript syntax to current ECMAScript standards.
